title: Wang2021 – Combined SSVEP dataset with single stimulus location for two inputs
description: A derivative EEG dataset implementing a combined steady-state visual evoked potential (SSVEP) brain-computer interface paradigm with spatially overlapping stimuli presented at a single location. The dataset comprises 8 healthy subjects performing a covert attention task to discriminate four arrow directions (up, down, left, right) flickering at distinct frequencies (7.73–14.17 Hz) derived from an 85 Hz CRT refresh rate. EEG signals were recorded at 1000 Hz using a 32-channel ANT Neuro system across two experimental schemes examining different attentional mechanisms.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired at 1000 Hz sampling rate using a 32-channel ANT Neuro eego mylab amplifier with standard 1005 montage. Each trial consisted of 500 ms fixation, 500 ms cue, and 5000 ms stimulation periods. Stimuli were overlapping SSVEP arrows presented at a single location on a CRT monitor (85 Hz refresh rate), with four frequency classes: 14.17 Hz, 12.14 Hz, 9.44 Hz, and 7.73 Hz.

affiliations.name: Department of Psychology and Behavioral Sciences Zhejiang University Hangzhou China
